security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.
Malicious actors can submit arbitrarily crafted vote data to influence proposals

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote data could be altered in transit without integrity checks.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Votes are transmitted without end-to-end integrity guarantees.

assumptions: The communication channel between the user and the governance contract is insecure.
The governance contract does not validate the integrity of incoming vote data.
An attacker has the ability to intercept and modify the vote data before it reaches the contract

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure communication channel (e.g., TLS) and data integrity checks.

description: An attacker may modify vote data in transit, potentially altering the outcome of a proposal.

security_gap.gap_description: The system lacks mechanisms to prevent vote repudiation.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not record or verify the origin of votes.

assumptions: The governance contract does not log the identity of voters.
The voting process does not employ mechanisms such as non-repudiation signatures to bind voters to their votes.
An attacker can cast votes on behalf of other users and subsequently deny their participation

mitigation.strategy: Implement a secure voting mechanism with verifiable audit trails and non-repudiation properties.

description: An attacker may cast votes on behalf of another user and later deny involvement.

security_gap.gap_description: The system is vulnerable to denial-of-service attacks.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Flooding the system with invalid or malformed vote requests could overwhelm the contract.

assumptions: The governance contract does not implement rate limiting or other anti-DoS measures.
An attacker can send a large volume of invalid or malformed vote requests to the governance contract.
This could cause the contract to become unresponsive or consume excessive resources

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting, input validation, and other anti-DoS mechanisms.

description: An attacker may flood the system with invalid votes, rendering the governance contract unavailable.
